sections.heading: Overview
sections.content: Deep links navigate users directly to specific app content. Android supports custom scheme links (myapp://), web deep links (HTTP/HTTPS), and Android App Links (verified HTTPS URLs opening directly in the app). App Links require domain verification via a Digital Asset Links JSON file and provide the highest security. Deep links are essential for notifications, marketing, QR codes, and web-to-app navigation.

rows.use_case: Public web-to-app, push notifications, QR codes

sections.heading: Manifest & Intent Filters
items: Declare intent filters on target Activity with VIEW action, DEFAULT and BROWSABLE categories
Use <data> elements with scheme, host, pathPrefix, path, pathPattern, autoVerify="true"
Combine multiple <data> elements for multiple URL patterns

sections.heading: Handling Deep Links
items: Retrieve Intent data in onCreate() and onNewIntent()
Parse path segments and query parameters safely
Validate and sanitize all URI input
Navigate using NavController or Navigation Component

sections.heading: Best Practices & Testing
items: Use App Links for public URLs with assetlinks.json verification
Include both debug and release keystore fingerprints
Test via adb: start Intents, check app link status, verify back stack
Avoid custom URI schemes for public links
Set launchMode or handle onNewIntent() to prevent duplicate Activities

sections.heading: Summary
sections.content: Use verified Android App Links for public-facing deep links, declare them in your navigation graph, handle both onCreate() and onNewIntent(), validate all parameters, and test thoroughly. Use NavDeepLinkBuilder for notifications and explicit deep links to build a correct back stack automatically. App Links ensure secure, seamless navigation from web, email, QR codes, and other apps.
